# Figure Description: Soviet-Era Borehole/Well Log

## Figure Type

This is a **borehole/well log (каротажная диаграмма)** — a composite geophysical well log typical of Soviet-era mineral and hydrocarbon exploration. It displays multiple geophysical measurement curves recorded along the depth axis of a drilled borehole, accompanied by a lithological column and supporting data tables. The format is consistent with standard Soviet geological survey logging practice from the 1970s–1980s.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The log appears to cover a **single borehole (скважина)**, likely one of the exploration/parametric wells drilled as part of the regional survey described in Report 36572. The header text (partially illegible due to scan quality) contains Cyrillic text consistent with a borehole identifier and location reference within map sheets **M-42 or N-42** of northern Kazakhstan.

- **Depth range**: The log extends from approximately **0 m at the top** to what appears to be **700–900+ metres** at the base of the main log section, with a separate continuation panel at the bottom of the image extending the record further (possibly to ~1,000–1,200 m total depth, though exact values are [illegible])
- The bottom panel appears to be a **depth extension or repeat section** of the same borehole, shown at a different scale or covering deeper intervals
- Formation names and stratigraphic designations are noted in the left-hand lithological margin but are largely [illegible] at this resolution

---

## Key Content

The figure is structured as a **multi-track composite log** with the following observable components, reading left to right:

### Left Margin — Lithological Column
- A **stratigraphic/lithological column** occupies the far left, showing varying rock textures rendered in standard Soviet geological symbols:
  - **Dotted patterns** suggesting sandstones or sandy horizons
  - **Brick/block patterns** suggesting limestones or consolidated sediments
  - **Fine horizontal hatching** suggesting clays or shales
  - **Coarser irregular patterns** suggesting conglomerates or altered crystalline rocks
- Depth markers are inscribed at regular intervals along this column (values [illegible] but spacing appears to be every 10 or 20 m)
- Formation or horizon labels appear in the left margin as Cyrillic text annotations, most [illegible]

### Central Tracks — Geophysical Curves
The dominant feature of the figure is **two or three overlapping continuous geophysical curves** running the full depth of the log. These show high-frequency variation throughout:

- **Left central curve**: Appears to be a **spontaneous potential (СП — самопроизвольная поляризация)** or **resistivity curve** — it shows moderate amplitude deflections, relatively smoother in character
- **Right central curve**: Shows higher amplitude, more erratic deflections consistent with a **lateral resistivity (КС — кажущееся сопротивление)** or **gamma-ray (ГК — гамма-каротаж)** log
- Both curves exhibit:
  - **High-amplitude anomalous zones** in the upper third (roughly 0–250 m equivalent depth), suggesting lithological contrast or mineralised intervals
  - **More subdued but persistent variation** through the middle section
  - **Renewed high-amplitude deflections** in the lower portion, which may correspond to basement or crystalline rock contact
- The curves are plotted on a **linear scale** with a baseline and scale divisions visible in the header (specific values [illegible])

### Right Side — Data Tables
- To the right of the curves is a structured **tabular section** with multiple narrow columns
- This likely records: **core recovery (%), interval depths, lithological descriptions, sample numbers, and possibly analytical results** (e.g., chemical assay or physical property values)
- Individual cell values are [illegible] at this resolution, but the column structure is clearly visible with entries at each logged interval
- Some numeric values are discernible in the rightmost columns but cannot be reliably transcribed

### Bottom Continuation Panel
- A **separate log panel** occupies the bottom ~15% of the image
- This appears to show a **deeper interval** or a **different logging run** (possibly with different tools or scale)
- The curves in this section show similarly complex variation
- Header text above this panel contains scale or interval information — partially readable as what may be depth references or tool designations ([illegible])

## Text and Labels

The following Cyrillic and numeric text is partially visible (transcribed as accurately as scan quality permits):

**Header (top of main log)**:
- Title area contains: **КАРОТАЖ** (Karotazh = Well Log) — visible centrally in the header band
- Subheadings include column labels that appear to read:
  - **«Буровые»** or similar (drilling-related column)
  - **«Интервал каротажа»** (logging interval)
  - **«Масштаб глубин»** (depth scale)
  - **«Описание пород»** (rock description)

## Research Value

This composite borehole log provides a **continuous record of subsurface lithology and petrophysical properties** for a specific drill hole in northern Kazakhstan, allowing direct characterisation of rock types, their depth distribution, and physical contrasts (resistivity, natural radioactivity, or spontaneous potential) that may indicate mineralised zones, aquifers, or stra
